title: Your future self has good news: you're doing pretty well
author: Grant St. Clair
summary:
How many times have you seen this setup: An older, grizzled version of a main character appears in a flash of light, hailing from a doomed future? They breathlessly deliver a message, optionally gesticulating wildly with a brand-new cybernetic limb, warning the protagonists not to make the same mistakes they did… and it gets old after, like, the third time. — Read the rest

The post
full_text:
How many times have you seen this setup: An older, grizzled version of a main character appears in a flash of light, hailing from a doomed future? They breathlessly deliver a message, optionally gesticulating wildly with a brand-new cybernetic limb, warning the protagonists not to make the same mistakes they did… and it gets old after, like, the third time.

Why aren't there any visitors from future timelines that are actually good? I mean, I assume it's because they're too busy actually enjoying that future utopia, but still, would it kill them to poke their head in and check in on their past selves. Actually, if Back to the Future is to be believed, it might. Masterful sketch comedians Chris and Jack have tackled this precise issue in their newest skit, where a grizzled future self pops in to say that… yeah, no, things are going pretty well, all things considered.

VIDEO

Considering this and their earlier skit about what a digital afterlife would actually be like, Chris and Jack are quickly becoming my favorite existential horror-focused sketch comedy duo composed of at least one former child voice actor on YouTube, which is not an accolade I hand out lightly.
